## Schmooooo...ing gemm dimensions

The goal of this performance dashboard generator is to schmoo across M, N and K for different data types, instructions and compare performance of CUTLASS and cuBLAS kernels.

## What happens when you run script?
There are two scripts to run.
1. `peak_perf_v2.py`, script runs `cutlass_profiler` and generate csv file with raw output from cutlass_profiler, one with just CUTLASS performance numbers and one with %SOL of both CUTLASS and cuBLAS.
2. `generate_viz.py`, script generates javascript file containing data for plotting %SOL comparison between CUTLASS and cuBLAS.

## How to run this script

Before running this script make sure to lock GPU clocks.

To run `peak_perf_v2.py` (pwd should be in ./tools/script/schmoo_gemm)
```
$ cd tools/scripts/schmoo_gemm
$ python3 peak_perf_v2.py --gpu-arch {gv100,tu102} \
  --profiler-path <path to cutlass_profiler> \
  --cuda-device-index <cuda device index of gpu> \
  --tags <csv tags you want to see in final perf data, optional> \
  --quick-mode <1|0 if you want to run largest problem size which takes less than exhaustive one> \
  --num-sms <number of sms in the gpu, required to calculate SOL of device> \
  --sm-clock <sm clock in MHz, required to calculate SOL of device> \
  --memory-clock <memory clock in MHz, required>
```

After running, csv data is created under `./assets/csv`

There are 4 kinds of csv files generated by the script.
1. perf-<gpu arch>-<op>-<layout>-<schmoo dimension>-all.csv, this file contains raw output from cutlass_profiler
2. perf-<gpu arch>-<op>-<layout>-<schmoo dimension>.csv, this file contains data from just CUTLASS provider
3. perf-<gpu arch>-<op>-<layout>-<schmoo dimension>-sol.csv, this file contains SOL data for CUTLASS and cuBLAS providers
4. performance-result.csv, this file contains summary of SOL, peak performance of CUTLASS for largest dimension for each op and layout.

Example: 

```
$ cd tools/scripts/schmoo_gemm
$ python3 peak_perf_v2.py --gpu-arch gv100 \
  --profiler-path ../../../build/tools/profiler \
  --cuda-device-index 0 \
  --tags "nvcc:10.2.83" \
  --quick-mode 1 \
  --num-sms 80 \
  --sm-clock 1335 \
  --memory-clock 850 

$ python3 ./peak_perf_v2.py --gpu-arch tu102 \
  --profiler-path ../../../build/tools/profiler \
  --cuda-device-index 1 \
  --tags "nvcc:10.2.83" \
  --quick-mode 0 \
  --num-sms 68 \
  --sm-clock 1650 \
  --memory-clock 6500
```

To run `generate_viz.py` (pwd should be in ./tools)

```python3 generate_viz.py --gpu-arch=<gv100/tu102>```
This script reads data from `./assets/csv` generated by `peak_perf_v2.py` and output to `./assets/data` which is later consumed by `.html` files in `./assets`
